issue_summary: Remove "SetView" from all graph implementations. See [] for why it should be killed with fire. In particular, it made the returned Sets have O(N) (in both time and memory) size() and contains() methods, and in one case, O(N^2) iteration!
javadoc_before:
/** * Returns the intersection of these two sets, using {@code Sets.intersection}: *
    *
  1. Outgoing edges of {@code node1}. *
  2. Incoming edges of {@code node2}. *
* The first argument passed to {@code Sets.intersection} is the smaller of the two sets. * * @see Sets#intersection */
javadoc_after:
/** * Returns the intersection of these two sets, using {@link Sets#intersection}: *
    *
  1. Outgoing edges of {@code node1}. *
  2. Incoming edges of {@code node2}. *
*/
